blocks.text: said it quietly. "Not enough." Julian didn't argue. But he didn't stop either.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: You know what you are in this situation.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: A civilian. No authority. No standing. No jurisdiction.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: If this becomes what I think it might become, it becomes mine. Not yours.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: You were outside that house last night when everyone else was inside. And you apparently don't miss much.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: The bracket. The window. The shape at 9:51. That's either very good instincts or very bad luck.
He almost smiled. He stopped himself, but it was there.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: Stay out of my way. Don't approach the five. Don't touch anything.

blocks.speaker: Inspector Davenport
blocks.content: You come to me. Not the other way around.

blocks.prompt: Davenport just gave Julian an opening. What does Julian take from this?

options.label: He trusts me enough to keep me close. That's leverage.
He's warning me as much as inviting me. Both things are true.
